title: Business School
weakest_section: 18:00-19:00 range with romantic tension overwhelming comedy

jokes.text: Oh, damn. I lost another file. Gonna have to reboot, again.
jokes.setup: Jim complains about computer problems
jokes.punchline: He's actually orchestrating a Pavlovian conditioning experiment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup that works retroactively. Jim's fake computer problems establish the bell sound for his Pavlovian experiment. Perfect character integration - only Jim would conduct this elaborate psychological experiment.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: My computer's been acting up all week. It freezes, the mouse doesn't work, it's a nightmare.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: But here's the thing — every time Dwight comes over to help, I've been hitting him with a stapler right before the computer suddenly works again.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So now whenever his computer glitches, he just walks over here expecting to get hit with office supplies. I've basically turned him into a computer troubleshooting dog.

jokes.text: In school, we learned about this scientist who trained dogs to salivate at the sound of a bell by feeding them whenever a bell rang. So, for the past couple weeks, I've been conducting a similar experiment.
jokes.setup: Jim references Pavlov's famous experiment in talking head
jokes.punchline: He's been conditioning Dwight like a laboratory animal
jokes.explanation: Exceptional concept execution. Takes a famous psychological experiment and applies it to office pranking. The formal, scientific language contrasting with the petty office prank is perfect Jim.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I've been conditioning Dwight like a laboratory animal. You know Pavlov? The guy with the dogs? I'm basically doing the same thing, except instead of a bell, I use compliments about beet farming, and instead of saliva, I get Dwight to do my work.

jokes.text: Altoid? Sure. Mint, Dwight? 'Mint, Dwight?' Yes.
jokes.setup: Computer dings, Jim offers mints
jokes.punchline: Dwight is now conditioned to expect mints at the computer sound
jokes.explanation: Strong execution of the experiment in action. The repetitive nature and Dwight's automatic responses build the comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Hey, want a mint?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Why would I want a mint?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I don't know, I just thought you might.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Well, I don't.

jokes.text: What are you doing? I... What? I don't know. I...
jokes.setup: Dwight realizes he's been conditioned
jokes.punchline: His confusion and inability to explain his behavior
jokes.explanation: Dwight's dawning realization and stammering confusion is perfectly in character and completes the experiment arc.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I have been... conditioned.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I don't know. I can't explain it. Every time I hear that sound, I just... I have to do it.

jokes.text: My mouth tastes so bad all of a sudden.
jokes.setup: Dwight has been conditioned to expect mints
jokes.punchline: Without the mint reward, his mouth 'tastes bad' - the conditioning worked
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff line. Dwight's mouth doesn't actually taste bad - he just expects the mint now. Brilliant psychological comedy with economy and character truth.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: My mouth tastes bad.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's because you're not getting your mint.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: What? No, that's ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: We conditioned you. Whenever you did something we wanted, we gave you a mint. Now your brain associates not getting a mint with something being wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's impossible. I'm not some kind of... Pavlov's dog.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Here. Have a mint.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Ahhh. Much better.

jokes.text: Always the bridesmaids, right, ladies?
jokes.setup: Angela commenting at wedding photo shoot
jokes.punchline: She's bitter about not being chosen for wedding party photos
jokes.explanation: Standard Angela cattiness. Works for character but not particularly clever or original.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I wasn't asked to be in the wedding party photos. Apparently I'm not 'photogenic' enough. Well, excuse me for having standards and not looking like I've been hit by a truck.

jokes.text: She has asked me to push her father's wheelchair down the aisle. So, basically, I am co-giving away the bride.
jokes.setup: Michael explains his wedding role
jokes.punchline: He inflates wheelchair pushing into 'co-giving away the bride'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael self-aggrandizement. Perfect character integration - only Michael would interpret this minor role so grandly.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not just going to push her down the aisle in a wheelchair. I'm going to be co-giving away the bride.

jokes.text: Since I pay her salary it is like I'm paying for the wedding, which I'm happy to do. It's a big day for Phyllis, but it's an even bigger day for me. Employer of the Bride.
jokes.setup: Michael continues explaining his importance
jokes.punchline: 'Employer of the Bride' as a twisted version of 'Father of the Bride'
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation of Michael's delusion. 'Employer of the Bride' is a genuinely funny twist on the traditional role, perfectly capturing his narcissism.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not the Father of the Bride, I'm the Employer of the Bride. Which is actually more important, because I'm the one who makes her money so she can afford the wedding in the first place.

jokes.text: It was the only way I could think to get six weeks off for my honeymoon. No one else has ever gotten six weeks before.
jokes.setup: Phyllis explains putting Michael in her wedding
jokes.punchline: She manipulated him for an unprecedented honeymoon length
jokes.explanation: Excellent revelation that reframes Michael's ego trip as Phyllis's calculated manipulation. Shows her as smarter than she appears.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: I manipulated Michael into giving me an unprecedented honeymoon length by putting him in my wedding.

jokes.text: Phyllis ended up using the exact same invitations as Roy and me. So it was kind of like being invited to my own wedding. And I was like, 'Wait, I thought I called that off?'
jokes.setup: Pam sees identical wedding invitations to her cancelled wedding
jokes.punchline: The surreal experience of being invited to her own cancelled wedding
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awkward concept. The psychological disorientation of seeing your cancelled wedding plans executed by someone else is genuinely unsettling and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I'm getting invited to my own cancelled wedding?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Well, technically it's not cancelled, it's just... postponed indefinitely.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Jim, that's the same thing.

jokes.text: So, what's in the box? A toaster. You? A toaster. Unbelievable.
jokes.setup: Two people comparing wedding gifts
jokes.punchline: They both brought the same generic gift
jokes.explanation: Standard wedding gift coincidence joke. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Oh my God, we got the same thing!

dialogue.speaker: Karen
dialogue.line: No way. What did you get?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: A Cuisinart food processor.

dialogue.speaker: Karen
dialogue.line: That's... exactly what I got.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Well, this is awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Karen
dialogue.line: I didn't even look at the registry.

jokes.text: You look as beautiful as the Queen of England. Thank you. Don't linger. Break left. Left.
jokes.setup: Dwight gives Angela a compliment at the wedding
jokes.punchline: He immediately gives her tactical movement instructions like a military operation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight behavior - treating a romantic moment like a covert operation. The contrast between romance and military tactics is well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Angela, you look absolutely stunning today.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Thank you, Dwight.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Now listen carefully. I need you to move to position two at 1400 hours. Keep your head down, avoid the cake table, and watch for Jim's attempts at small talk. If Michael approaches, execute evasive maneuvers immediately.

jokes.text: The Schrutes have their own traditions. We usually marry standing in our own graves. It makes the funerals very romantic, but the weddings are a bleak affair.
jokes.setup: Dwight explains Schrute wedding traditions
jokes.punchline: They marry in their own graves, making funerals romantic but weddings bleak
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy. The twisted logic of making funerals romantic while weddings become bleak is brilliantly absurd and perfectly Dwight. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: They marry in their own graves, making funerals romantic but weddings bleak

jokes.text: 'P and R'? Phyllis and Robert? Oh, of course. Also, Pam and Roy.
jokes.setup: Jim and Karen notice the monogram on the flowers
jokes.punchline: Awkward realization it also matches Pam and Roy's cancelled wedding
jokes.explanation: Solid awkward moment that reinforces the theme of Phyllis copying Pam's wedding plans. Jim's realization creates appropriate discomfort.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Wait, those initials... P.R.

dialogue.speaker: Karen
dialogue.line: Yeah, so? It's a nice monogram.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: P.R. Pam and Roy.

dialogue.speaker: Karen
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: These are the flowers from her cancelled wedding.

jokes.text: I swear, Phyllis, you are as beautiful as the first day you started work at Dunder Mifflin. Thanks, Michael. That's sweet. Same as when you said it outside.
jokes.setup: Michael gives Phyllis a heartfelt compliment
jokes.punchline: Phyllis reveals he already said the exact same thing outside
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's repetitive nature and Phyllis's gentle deflation of his moment.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Phyllis, I just want you to know that you are a truly special person. Your kindness and grace make this office a better place.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Thank you, Michael. Actually, you said that exact same thing to me outside five minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I did?

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Word for word.

jokes.text: If you need to vomit, that is okay. I did. Do you want to talk about tonight? No. Because I know you're probably worried about pleasing Bob. A lot of pressure.
jokes.setup: Michael tries to comfort nervous bride Phyllis
jokes.punchline: He brings up his own vomiting and sexual performance anxiety
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate oversharing. His attempt at comfort becomes awkward sexual discussion.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Phyllis, listen to me. I understand you're nervous. On my wedding day, I was so anxious I threw up three times.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: That's not helping.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And then, you know, when it came time to, uh, consummate the marriage, I couldn't... I just couldn't perform.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Michael, stop.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But you know what? I got through it. And so will you.

jokes.text: Phyllis, did you break wind? It's okay if you did, it's a very natural reaction. It's your wedding, and you're nervous... That wasn't me. Okay.
jokes.setup: Someone farts in the room
jokes.punchline: Michael assumes it's nervous bride Phyllis, but it wasn't her
jokes.explanation: Basic fart humor. Michael's assumption and formal language around bodily functions is mildly funny but not sophisticated.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, it's alright. Pre-wedding jitters, nothing to be embarrassed about. I once sharted myself before my first wedding.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Michael, that wasn't me.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm not judging. It happens to the best of us.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: No, I mean it literally wasn't me. That was Kevin.

jokes.text: I'm sure that Bob... Wow, that is... That is pungent. I lost my train of thought.
jokes.setup: Michael continues talking despite the smell
jokes.punchline: The fart smell overwhelms him and derails his speech
jokes.explanation: Extension of the fart joke. Michael's formal reaction ('pungent') has mild humor but it's still basic toilet humor.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: As I was saying, the key to successful management is maintaining composure and authority in any situation, no matter what obstacles—

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: —what is that smell? Did someone... is that...

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Oh my God, that is foul. That is absolutely—I can't even— *fans face* Who did this? This is... I've lost my train of thought completely.

jokes.text: Are you set on that hairstyle? I thought it was... Michael, no... Here, let me. I don't need your help. Thank you, no. Just cover up that bald patch. Michael, please. I just need some time alone.
jokes.setup: Michael tries to help Phyllis with her hair
jokes.punchline: He criticizes her hairstyle and points out her bald patch
jokes.explanation: Michael's tactless 'help' creating discomfort. His inability to read social cues is in character.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Phyllis, I'm going to be honest with you. This hairstyle? Not working. And what's going on back here?

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You've got a bald patch. A bald patch, Phyllis.

jokes.text: You might be surprised to learn that I've only been to one other wedding. It's actually a very cute story. My mom was marrying Jeff, and they asked me to be ring bearer. And I was understandably emotional and somehow my pants became wet.
jokes.setup: Michael shares his only other wedding experience
jokes.punchline: As a child ring bearer, he wet his pants from emotion
jokes.explanation: Good character backstory that explains Michael's wedding anxiety. The childhood trauma adds depth to his behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I was a ring bearer once. At my cousin's wedding. I was so emotional, I wet my pants.

jokes.text: I hate you! A long story short, Jeff's dog ended up as ring bearer. And the irony is that, after the ceremony that dog peed on everything and nobody said boo.
jokes.setup: Young Michael was replaced by a dog as ring bearer
jokes.punchline: The dog peed everywhere but nobody cared, unlike when Michael wet himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the ring bearer story. The bitter irony of the dog being allowed to pee while Michael was shamed for it is perfectly constructed.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: The dog peed everywhere but nobody cared, unlike when Michael wet himself.

jokes.text: Why are all these people here? There's too many people on this Earth. We need a new plague.
jokes.setup: Dwight complains about the wedding crowd
jokes.punchline: He advocates for a plague to reduce population
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight dark humor. His casual genocidal solution to overcrowding is shockingly funny and perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: This wedding is too crowded. We need a plague. A good plague would really thin things out. Decimate the population. I'm not saying I want people to die, I'm just saying if it happened, it would solve a lot of problems.

jokes.text: I saw Wedding Crashers accidentally. I bought a ticket for Grizzly Man and went into the wrong theater. After an hour, I figured I was in the wrong theater, but I kept waiting. Because that's the thing about bear attacks, they come when you least expect it.
jokes.setup: Creed explains seeing Wedding Crashers by accident
jokes.punchline: He stayed in the wrong movie for an hour waiting for bear attacks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Creed moment. His logic about bear attacks being unexpected perfectly explains why he'd wait through a romantic comedy. The absurd patience is wonderfully in character.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: I went to see Wedding Crashers by accident. I thought it was about actual wedding crashes, like car crashes at weddings. Stayed in the theater for an hour waiting for the bear attacks.

jokes.text: Once again, Jim, I will take care of this. I will locate the wedding crashers and report them to Phyllis. That way I won't have to get her a gift.
jokes.setup: Dwight volunteers to find wedding crashers
jokes.punchline: His real motivation is avoiding buying a wedding gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - turning his duty into personal benefit. The mercenary motivation disguised as helpfulness is well-crafted.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I will find the wedding crashers. It is my duty.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's... actually really helpful, Dwight.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Of course it is. And as a bonus, I don't have to buy a wedding gift.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Wait, what?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Security duty exempts me from gift-giving obligations. It's in the employee handbook. Section 4, subsection B.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Kevin. Where did you find her? At the gym. Right. The gym...
jokes.setup: Kevin talks to a wedding guest about his date
jokes.punchline: Kevin's skeptical reaction suggests the gym story is implausible
jokes.explanation: Kevin's dubious reaction implies the couple met somewhere less respectable than a gym. His tone suggests he knows better.

jokes.text: Could you scoot over? You're on my dress. I thought you're not supposed to wear white to a wedding. I know, but there was an emergency. I look really good in white.
jokes.setup: Someone questions Angela wearing white to a wedding
jokes.punchline: Her 'emergency' excuse is just that she looks good in white
jokes.explanation: Angela's vanity overriding wedding etiquette is perfectly in character. Her fake emergency excuse is amusingly shallow.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: Angela, you're wearing white to a wedding?

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: It's an emergency.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: What emergency?

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I look good in white.

jokes.text: This strapping, young lad sitting here is Phyllis' father, Elbert, and he is quite the ladies' man. Aren't you, Elbert? Huh? Ring bearer. I could have done better. I will do better. I am going to be better.
jokes.setup: Michael introduces Phyllis's elderly father
jokes.punchline: He's still obsessing over his childhood ring bearer failure
jokes.explanation: Michael's unresolved childhood trauma surfacing inappropriately. His determination to prove himself is pathetically endearing.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Everyone, this is Phyllis's father, Bob Vance's... I mean, Phyllis's father.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis's Father
dialogue.line: I was supposed to be ring bearer at my cousin's wedding in 1987, but I wasn't chosen.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And he's still not over it.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis's Father
dialogue.line: They picked my brother instead. I was available. I had the time.

jokes.text: That's my dress.
jokes.setup: Pam sees Phyllis in her wedding dress
jokes.punchline: It's literally the same dress Pam picked for her cancelled wedding
jokes.explanation: Devastating three-word line. The shock of seeing someone else wearing your wedding dress is perfectly captured. Maximum impact with minimal words.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: It's literally the same dress I picked for my cancelled wedding.

jokes.text: It's...
jokes.setup: Michael sees Phyllis's father stand up during the ceremony
jokes.punchline: He's speechless as his moment is stolen
jokes.explanation: Michael's moment of defeat. His inability to complete a sentence shows his deflation as his big moment is upstaged.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I was gonna do the father of the bride speech. That was my moment. That was MY moment.

jokes.text: Me walking Phyllis down the aisle was supposed to be the highlight of the wedding. And now, the wedding has no highlight.
jokes.setup: Michael explains his disappointment about the ceremony
jokes.punchline: He believes the wedding has NO highlight without his participation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael narcissism. He can't conceive that a wedding could have meaning without him at the center. The total self-absorption is beautifully crafted.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I was really looking forward to this wedding. I thought it was going to be the highlight of the year. But then I realized... without me there, there is no highlight. There's just... a ceremony.

jokes.text: I can't believe I pushed that guy's lazy ass around all day until he was ready to stand up and steal the show.
jokes.setup: Michael's continued bitterness about being upstaged
jokes.punchline: He calls a disabled man 'lazy' for using a wheelchair
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate anger at a disabled man is both horrible and funny. His obliviousness to the absurdity is in character.

jokes.text: Well, I've got news for you, Elbert, if that's your real name, the show's not over.
jokes.setup: Michael's building anger at being upstaged
jokes.punchline: He questions the elderly man's name and threatens retaliation
jokes.explanation: Michael treating an elderly father like a criminal conspiracy is absurd. The fake name suspicion shows his paranoid state.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: What is your name?

dialogue.speaker: Elderly Man
dialogue.line: Gerald.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, Gerald, you just made a big mistake.

jokes.text: And do you, Phyllis, take Bob Vance, Vance Refrigeration, to be your lawfully wedded husband? I do. Ladies and gentlemen, may I present to you, for the first time as a couple, Mr. And Mrs. Bob Vance!
jokes.setup: Michael takes over the wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: He's doing it out of order and prematurely announcing them married
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperate attempt to regain control by hijacking the ceremony. His incompetence makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: By the power vested in me by the state of Pennsylvania, I now pronounce you husband and wife!

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Michael, we haven't even exchanged vows yet.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, too late now. You're married.

jokes.text: And do you, Bob, take Phyllis to be your lawfully wedded wife? I do. You may now kiss the bride. Ladies and gentlemen, for the first time as a couple, Mr. And Mrs. Bob Vance!
jokes.setup: The real officiant tries to continue properly
jokes.punchline: The ceremony proceeds normally despite Michael's interruption
jokes.explanation: The contrast between Michael's chaos and the ceremony continuing normally. Shows his irrelevance.

dialogue.speaker: Officiant
dialogue.line: We are gathered here today to witness the union of these two souls in matrimony.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

dialogue.speaker: Officiant
dialogue.line: I will now ask if there are any objections to this union.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: No objections here.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: None from me.

dialogue.speaker: Officiant
dialogue.line: Then by the power vested in me, I now pronounce you husband and wife. You may kiss the bride.

jokes.text: That's what I'm talking about!
jokes.setup: The ceremony concludes
jokes.punchline: Michael takes credit for the successful ceremony
jokes.explanation: Michael's shameless credit-taking despite disrupting the ceremony. His obliviousness continues.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, folks, I think we can all agree that was the most beautiful ceremony this office has ever seen. And you know who made that happen? This guy. *points to self* Michael Gary Scott. I orchestrated every detail, from the music selection to the lighting. Flawless execution.

jokes.text: Your dress is very white. It's so white, my eyes are burning. Thanks, Angela.
jokes.setup: Angela compliments Phyllis's dress
jokes.punchline: Her 'compliment' is actually criticism disguised as praise
jokes.explanation: Angela's passive-aggressive 'compliment' is perfectly in character. She's bitter about not being the center of attention.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: That dress is very... flattering. It really minimizes your... proportions.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Thank you!

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: And the color is so bold. It really takes courage to wear something that loud when you have your... coloring.

jokes.text: But just know, if you ever lay a finger on Phyllis, I will kill you. If you ever lay a finger on Phyllis, I'll kill you. Agreed. No fingers will be laid on Phyllis.
jokes.setup: Michael threatens Bob about treating Phyllis well
jokes.punchline: Jim repeats it, then Bob agrees to the literal interpretation of 'no fingers'
jokes.explanation: The escalation from protective threat to absurd literal interpretation. Bob's confused compliance adds to the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Bob, if you ever hurt Phyllis, I will break every bone in your body.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: If you ever hurt Phyllis, I will break every bone in your body.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Okay, I won't use my fingers.

jokes.text: Oh, decided to sit down again, huh? Great. Bet you can hear me, too?
jokes.setup: Michael sees Phyllis's father back in his wheelchair
jokes.punchline: He sarcastically assumes the man is faking his disability
jokes.explanation: Michael's continued bitterness leads him to question a disabled man's legitimacy. His pettiness is both awful and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh, come on. You're not even trying anymore. I can see your toes curling. You're faking it.

jokes.text: Also, I'm gonna need to see a copy of the guest manifest as well as photographs of the caterers. I don't have that, Dwight. Damn it, Phyllis!
jokes.setup: Dwight continues his wedding crasher investigation
jokes.punchline: He demands impossible documentation and curses at the bride
jokes.explanation: Dwight treating a wedding like a crime scene and cursing at the bride is perfectly absurd. His dedication to the wrong priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I need to see your marriage license, birth certificate, and a notarized letter from your parents confirming you're actually human.

dialogue.speaker: Bride
dialogue.line: What? This is my wedding!

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Not until I verify your credentials. This is a serious matter.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: And frankly, this whole thing reeks of fraud. You can go to hell.

jokes.text: This must be so awful for you. What do you mean? Well, this is supposed to be your wedding. Oh! No, that's actually fine. There's no way it's fine. I'm sorry. If I was you, I would just, like, freak out and get really drunk and then tell someone I was pregnant.
jokes.setup: Karen sympathizes with Pam about the wedding similarities
jokes.punchline: She suggests increasingly dramatic responses ending with fake pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Karen's escalating suggestions for wedding drama culminate in the absurd fake pregnancy announcement. Good escalation structure.

dialogue.speaker: Karen
dialogue.line: You know, I totally get it. My wedding was similar to yours.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Karen
dialogue.line: Yeah. So here's what you do. First, tell everyone it was perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Karen
dialogue.line: Then, if they keep bringing it up, tell them you're thinking about renewing your vows.

dialogue.speaker: Karen
dialogue.line: If that doesn't work, fake a pregnancy.

jokes.text: Excuse me, sir, how do you know the happy couple? Who? The bride and groom. What are their names? I don't... I'm not sure... I get it. I get it. Come on, freeloader. Let's move it.
jokes.setup: Dwight interrogates a suspected wedding crasher
jokes.punchline: The man can't name the bride and groom, confirming Dwight's suspicion
jokes.explanation: Dwight actually successfully identifies a real wedding crasher. His detective work pays off, though his methods are absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Name the bride and groom. Now.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown guest
dialogue.line: Um... Brad and... Jennifer?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Wrong. You're a wedding crasher.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown guest
dialogue.line: No, I'm not—

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Security!

jokes.text: Why don't you find your seat and enjoy the buffet. I'm already on it. The chicken? Totally undercooked. I sent it back. It's fish. I will take care of that.
jokes.setup: Michael reports food problems to the bride
jokes.punchline: He sent back fish thinking it was undercooked chicken
jokes.explanation: Michael's food expertise fails spectacularly when he can't distinguish fish from chicken. His confidence makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yeah, I sent back the fish because I thought it was undercooked chicken.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: That was fish, Michael.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, it was very poorly cooked.

jokes.text: I do. I know a fair amount about fine food and drink. This is a white.
jokes.setup: Michael claims wine expertise
jokes.punchline: His 'expert' assessment is just stating the obvious color
jokes.explanation: Michael's pretentious wine knowledge is exposed as completely basic. His confidence in stating the obvious is endearingly stupid.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: This is a excellent Pinot Noir. You can tell by the color. It's... red.

jokes.text: This is not our first wedding. This is the third wedding that Scrantonicity has played. We also played our bassist's wedding and our guitarist's wedding.
jokes.setup: Kevin explains the band's wedding experience
jokes.punchline: Their entire wedding resume is playing their own members' weddings
jokes.explanation: Kevin's matter-of-fact delivery about their limited wedding gig history is perfectly deadpan. The band only plays for themselves.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Their entire wedding resume is playing their own members' weddings

jokes.text: Attention, everyone. Attention, please. I'm supposed to ask if anyone has seen Uncle Al. He is old and has brown eyes and dementia. His family is very concerned. It's a very serious situation.
jokes.setup: Michael makes an announcement about a missing person
jokes.punchline: He treats a dementia patient's disappearance as a casual wedding announcement
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate venue for a missing person announcement. The contrast between party atmosphere and serious situation.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Attention everyone, I have an announcement to make. Meredith's mother has gone missing.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But before we all panic, I want to say that Meredith's mother, Meredith's mother, is no longer with us. She has left the building. She is missing.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So if everyone could just keep an eye out... Actually, you know what? Let's make this fun. It's like a scavenger hunt, but for a person with dementia.

jokes.text: I know I don't normally notice these kind of things, but this wedding's really nice. I mean, the flowers and stuff. Phyllis has got some great taste. You're kidding me, right?
jokes.setup: Roy compliments the wedding decorations
jokes.punchline: Pam is incredulous because these were her ideas that Phyllis stole
jokes.explanation: Roy's obliviousness to his own cancelled wedding plans creates painful irony. Pam's frustration is palpable.

dialogue.speaker: Roy
dialogue.line: These decorations look great.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Wait, what? Roy, these are my ideas. Phyllis stole them from me.

dialogue.speaker: Roy
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, they still look great.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: That's not the point!

jokes.text: I know you probably aren't gonna remember this, but, those color roses, I got you those color roses for our prom. Roy, I picked those flowers. Phyllis just stole all of my ideas for our wedding.
jokes.setup: Roy thinks he remembers getting Pam those roses
jokes.punchline: Pam corrects him - she picked everything and Phyllis copied it all
jokes.explanation: Roy's false memory being corrected by Pam reveals both his inattentiveness and the depth of Phyllis's copying. Good character work.

dialogue.speaker: Roy
dialogue.line: Yeah, I remember getting you those roses.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Roy, you didn't get me anything. I picked everything out myself. And then Phyllis copied the whole thing.

dialogue.speaker: Roy
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: I guess I wasn't really too involved in the planning. Yeah. Sorry about that. It's okay. Well, you think it sucks for you? I'm the one who actually wanted to get married.
jokes.setup: Roy apologizes for not being involved in wedding planning
jokes.punchline: Pam reveals she was the one who wanted marriage while watching someone else have her wedding
jokes.explanation: Devastating moment where Pam reveals the core truth - she wanted marriage while Roy didn't, and now she's watching her dream played out by someone else.

dialogue.speaker: Roy
dialogue.line: I'm sorry I haven't been more involved in the wedding planning.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: It's fine. I wanted to marry you. I just didn't want to plan a wedding with you.

jokes.text: Phyllis, you're a wonderful woman. And you're a hell of a bowler! Cheers. Thank you, Randy. That was great. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Random guest gives heartfelt toast
jokes.punchline: His compliment is about Phyllis being good at bowling
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing that the toast focuses on bowling skills. Sets up contrast with Michael's elaborate speech to come.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That was beautiful. Really touching.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, what did he say about Phyllis?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That she's good at bowling? That's the compliment?

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Michael Scott. And, for the next 40 minutes, I am going to be your tour guide through the lives of Phyllis Lapin and Bob Vance, one of the great, seemingly impossible, love stories of our time.
jokes.setup: Michael begins his wedding speech
jokes.punchline: He announces it will last 40 minutes and compares them to great love stories
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael opening - way too long, overly grandiose, and completely inappropriate. The 40-minute warning is particularly horrifying.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: This speech is going to last 40 minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And I'm going to compare Jim and Pam's love story to some of the greatest love stories of all time.

jokes.text: Webster's dictionary defines wedding as 'the fusing of two metals with a hot torch.' Well, you know something? I think you guys are two medals, gold medals.
jokes.setup: Michael quotes dictionary definition of welding, not wedding
jokes.punchline: He pivots to calling them 'medals' instead of metals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael mistake - confusing welding with wedding, then trying to save it with the medals/metals pun. Classic Michael speech disaster with unexpected wordplay redemption.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Welding is the process of joining metal parts by melting them together.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, I meant... medals. They're giving out medals.

jokes.text: The most important part of a speech is the opening line. When time is not a factor, I like to try out three or four different ones.
jokes.setup: Michael explains his speech philosophy in talking head
jokes.punchline: He admits to trying multiple openings when time permits
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on his speech-giving process. The casual admission of multiple attempts shows his dedication to being the center of attention.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I have a very specific philosophy when it comes to giving speeches. I like to start with a joke to break the ice, then I go into my core material. But if I have time, I'll try multiple openings to see which one gets the biggest laugh.

jokes.text: Phyllis and Bob, their celebrity couple name would be Flob.
jokes.setup: Michael creates a celebrity couple name
jokes.punchline: Flob is an unsexy and unfortunate combination
jokes.explanation: The unfortunate sound of 'Flob' makes it amusing. Michael's attempt at modern celebrity culture falls flat appropriately.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Flob is an unsexy and unfortunate combination.

jokes.text: You look at her, and she's kind of matronly today, but back in high school, I swear her nickname was Easy Rider.
jokes.setup: Michael comments on Phyllis's current appearance
jokes.punchline: He reveals an embarrassing alleged high school nickname at her wedding
jokes.explanation: Extremely inappropriate wedding speech content. Michael's tactlessness reaches new heights by insulting the bride and revealing sexual history.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Phyllis, you look absolutely stunning today.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, back in high school, people used to call you 'Phyl-licious,' but that's not what I remember.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I remember when they called you 'Phyllis the Paralysis' because you were so shy you couldn't move.

jokes.text: I didn't say anything when Phyllis's dad upstaged me at the ceremony. And I think you owe me this, okay? Give me the microphone. No, I'm not going to. Michael, give me the microphone. It's okay. You're out of here.
jokes.setup: Michael refuses to give up the microphone
jokes.punchline: He cites being upstaged by a disabled father as grounds for speech rights
jokes.explanation: Michael's logic that he deserves compensation for being upstaged by the bride's father is perfectly selfish and absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not giving up this microphone. Do you know what it's like to be upstaged by a disabled father? That's my thing!

jokes.text: I hate you!
jokes.setup: Michael is being removed from giving his speech
jokes.punchline: He shouts his hatred like a child having a tantrum
jokes.explanation: Michael's complete breakdown into childish rage. The simplicity and venom of the line captures his immaturity perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I hate you! I hate you! I hate you all!

jokes.text: When are we gonna get to see some of those famous Beesly dance moves? I'm pacing myself. Come on. Get out there. Give the people what they want. No. I'm such a dorky dancer. I know. And it's very cute.
jokes.setup: Jim encourages Pam to dance
jokes.punchline: He calls her dorky dancing 'very cute' in a flirtatious way
jokes.explanation: Sweet Jim-Pam moment. His appreciation of her awkward dancing shows his genuine affection beyond surface attraction.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Come on, dance with me.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: No, I'm not a good dancer.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yes, you are. That's very cute.

jokes.text: Hypothetically, if I thought Pam was interested, then, no, it's totally hypothetical.
jokes.setup: Jim starts to express feelings about Pam to camera
jokes.punchline: He catches himself and unconvincingly claims it's hypothetical
jokes.explanation: Jim's internal struggle with his feelings for Pam. His inability to maintain the hypothetical facade shows his true feelings.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I mean, if I were to ever... you know, express my feelings to Pam, I think she would... I mean, hypothetically speaking, she might appreciate the honesty, but that's just a theory I have, nothing based on actual... I'm not actually planning to do that.

jokes.text: Come on. I can't let you in, Michael. Dwight, just... No. It's Bob and Phyllis's orders. Look, I just wanted to go in and quietly sit, and have a piece of cake. I'm not even gonna dance, one song, maybe...
jokes.setup: Michael is banned from the reception and begging Dwight to let him in
jokes.punchline: His 'quiet' plan immediately includes dancing 'one song, maybe'
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to stick to his own modest promises. Even while begging, he can't resist adding conditions that prove he'll cause more trouble.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight, come on, just let me in. I'll be quiet, I promise.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: No, Michael. You're banned.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'll just do one song. Maybe. Just one song and I'll be quiet.

jokes.text: You are a real life wedding crasher and I must bounce you. I'm sorry, it gives me no pleasure.
jokes.setup: Dwight explains why he can't let Michael in
jokes.punchline: He calls his own boss a wedding crasher and claims no pleasure in bouncing him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight loyalty conflict - duty to the couple overrides loyalty to Michael. His formal security language is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Michael, I cannot let you in. You are not on the list.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: You are a wedding crasher. And I take no pleasure in this, but I must bounce you.

jokes.text: Hey! They're playing our song, huh? Yeah, that's weird. I thought they only played The Police. I know. I... I gave them 20 bucks.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam hear 'their song' at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Jim reveals he paid the band to play it
jokes.explanation: Sweet romantic gesture reveals Jim's planning and investment in the moment with Pam. His confession is endearingly nervous.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Is that... our song?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah. I may have paid the band a little extra to play it.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: You didn't.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I did. For you.

jokes.text: You wanna dance?
jokes.setup: Jim asks Pam to dance to their special song
jokes.punchline: The weight of the romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Not traditionally funny but creates romantic tension. The simplicity carries emotional weight in context of their relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Hey, remember our song?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Of course I do.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dance with me?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Right now? Here?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah. Come on.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I love you, Pam.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I love you too.

jokes.text: Hey, you wanna get out of here?
jokes.setup: After dancing intimately with Pam
jokes.punchline: Jim suggests leaving together
jokes.explanation: The culmination of romantic tension. Not a traditional joke but creates dramatic comedy through relationship awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: We should probably get out of here.

jokes.text: Here's a non-hypothetical, I'm really happy I'm with Karen.
jokes.setup: Jim addresses his earlier hypothetical comment about Pam
jokes.punchline: He claims happiness with Karen immediately after his intimate moment with Pam
jokes.explanation: Jim's defensive overcompensation after being tempted by Pam. The 'non-hypothetical' callback while he's clearly conflicted creates dramatic irony.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Look, I was just making a hypothetical comment.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Karen and I are perfectly happy together.

jokes.text: Toby... Yeah! I just want Phyllis to have a great day. Phyllis and you will be great together. We are great together. We are a great team. The Celtics were a great team. Yes. Yes, they were. Robert Parish...
jokes.setup: Toby tries to console Michael about being kicked out
jokes.punchline: Michael's rambling about basketball teams shows his emotional state
jokes.explanation: Michael's scattered emotional state leads to random basketball tangent. His vulnerability makes Toby's kindness more poignant.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Michael, I know this is hard. But you need to talk about your feelings.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: My feelings? Fine. The Lakers are going nowhere. The Celtics are a bunch of has-beens. And don't even get me started on the Knicks. They're a disaster. A complete disaster.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Michael, I think you're displacing your—

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And the Heat? Overrated. The Spurs are boring. The Warriors are too young. And the Cavaliers... the Cavaliers have no business being in the league.

jokes.text: I should talk to her. I don't want this to ruin her honeymoon. Well, nobody ever helped me. I had to do it myself. Even the doctor didn't know. Dude, keep it together.
jokes.setup: Michael wants to apologize to Phyllis
jokes.punchline: He starts rambling about personal medical issues
jokes.explanation: Michael's emotional breakdown includes inappropriate oversharing. Toby's 'keep it together' is a rare moment of him being the voice of reason.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Phyllis, I want to apologize. I'm sorry for what I said. Look, I've been dealing with a lot lately. My lower back has been killing me, and I think it's because I sleep on my stomach, but my doctor says I should sleep on my side, but that hurts my shoulder. And then there's my acid reflux, which has been acting up because I've been stress eating, and—

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Michael, what does this have to do with anything?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm getting there! The point is, I'm sorry. My body is falling apart, and I took it out on you.

jokes.text: Phyllis? Phyllis, wait, please. I'm sorry. I just... I just wanted to make this a day to remember. You found Uncle Al! Yeah, yeah. He's kind of a weirdo. Thank you, Michael.
jokes.setup: Michael apologizes to Phyllis for ruining her wedding
jokes.punchline: She's grateful he found the missing dementia patient
jokes.explanation: Michael accidentally redeems himself by finding Uncle Al. His casual 'kind of a weirdo' shows he doesn't understand the gravity.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Phyllis, I am so sorry for ruining your wedding. I feel terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Actually, Michael, I'm grateful. You found that missing dementia patient.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, he was just wandering around the reception in his hospital gown.

jokes.text: They say that your wedding day goes by in such a flash that you're lucky if you even get a piece of your own cake. I say, that's crazy. I say, 'Let them eat cake.' Margaret Thatcher said that about marriage. Smart broad.
jokes.setup: Michael shares wedding wisdom
jokes.punchline: He misattributes Marie Antoinette's famous quote to Margaret Thatcher and marriage
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael historical confusion. Mixing up Marie Antoinette with Margaret Thatcher and applying it to marriage shows his scattered knowledge.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, I read that Marie Antoinette said 'Let them eat cake.' Actually, I think that was Margaret Thatcher. Anyway, that's what marriage is all about - letting them eat cake.

jokes.text: Be careful with the... No! Oh, wow! Phyllis. Phyllis, come on. You look like a clown. Here. Oh, get me. Get me!
jokes.setup: Someone warns about cake cutting
jokes.punchline: Cake gets smashed, Michael calls Phyllis a clown, then wants to be included in the mess
jokes.explanation: Standard wedding cake disaster. Michael's tactless 'clown' comment followed by wanting to join in is typically selfish.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: You have to be very careful when you cut the cake, or it'll fall apart.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm just kidding. But seriously, how hard can it be to cut a cake?

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: It's harder than it looks.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, you're a clown.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Excuse me?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I mean, can I get in on this? I want to help destroy the cake too.

tmdb_overview: Roy stopped by Pam's desk to let her know how excited he was to see her art show. She reminded him that it was just a small show being put on by her art class. Pam later admitted she was very happy she was back together with Roy because it showed maturity. Jim feigns indifference to the reconciliation.
insight: Michael's desperation to impress Pam's class generates 69 jokes in 23 minutes of relentless cringe.
